title: Life expectancy gap for Black Bostonians is growing, health officials warn
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: The life expectancy gap for Black Bostonians has doubled from 3.3 to 6.6 years since 2013.
Black Bostonians have an average life expectancy of 76.2 years, compared to 82.2 years for others.
Overdose, cancer, and cardiometabolic disease are leading causes of premature mortality for Black residents.

title: Former Celtics center Luke Kornet calls out Hawks over ‘Magic City’ promotion
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Luke Kornet criticizes Atlanta Hawks' 'Magic City Night' promotion due to its association with a strip club.
Kornet urges the NBA community to reconsider the promotion, stressing respect and safety for women.
Hawks' promotion includes a performance by rapper T.I. and serves Magic City's lemon pepper wings.

title: Married couple from R.I. identified as victims in fatal Swansea crash
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Carolyn and James Carcasi, a married couple from Bristol, R.I., died in a Swansea car crash on February 27.
The crash at Routes 136 and 6 involved an Infiniti driven by Demitri Sousa and the couple’s Subaru Ascent.
Sousa, 28, is suspected in a Rhode Island murder earlier that evening and is hospitalized with serious injuries.

title: Steve Kerr apologizes for comments about Kristaps Porzingis’ lingering illness
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Steve Kerr apologized for comments on Kristaps Porzingis' illness, acknowledging he is not qualified to discuss the diagnosis.
Porzingis, diagnosed with postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome, was limited to 18 games this season with the Warriors and Hawks.
Kerr clarified Porzingis was kept home due to a separate illness, not POTS, citing he was losing fluids and contagious.

title: Taking stock of Bruins’ top trade chips entering the NHL deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins are evaluating trade options using draft picks and players like Mason Lohrei before the March 6 deadline.
The Bruins hold four first-round draft picks over the next two years, including Toronto's 2026 selection, as potential trade assets.
Players like Andrew Peeke, Viktor Arvidsson, and Casey Mittelstadt could be part of trade discussions to improve the Bruins' roster.

title: NFL insider: Maxx Crosby trade could happen ‘this week’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Maxx Crosby could be traded this week, with Patriots among teams interested, according to insider Albert Breer.
Las Vegas Raiders are reportedly receiving interest from multiple teams for Maxx Crosby, says NFL Media’s Tom Pelissero.
The NFL league year and free agency signing period begin on March 11, possibly influencing Crosby trade timing.

title: Quincy man sentenced in fatal St. Patrick’s Day assault in downtown Boston
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Sanusi Sadiq, 31, sentenced to 3-6 years for manslaughter in the death of Barry Whelan in Boston.
Whelan, 46, died from a brain injury after a St. Patrick’s Day 2023 altercation with Sadiq on Winter Street.
Sadiq's retrial concluded with a guilty verdict; initial trial in February 2025 ended in a mistrial.

title: Bill Simmons seems convinced that ‘all signs are pointing to’ this date for Jayson Tatum’s return
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Bill Simmons suggests Jayson Tatum may return Friday against the Mavericks at TD Garden.
The Celtics have not announced a timeline for Tatum's return following his achilles tendon repair.
Tatum has been cleared for 5-on-5 practice, but remains out for Tuesday's game against the Bucks.

title: Should teens be allowed to carry handguns in Massachusetts?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The Massachusetts Supreme Judicial Court will hear a case on whether those under 21 can legally carry handguns.
Gun-rights advocates argue the state's age restriction on handgun licenses violates the Second Amendment.
The case originated from Mikai Thomson's conviction after being found with a handgun at age 20.

title: 5 takeaways from Don Sweeney’s Bruins press conference before trade deadline
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins GM Don Sweeney plans strategic trade deadline moves without dismantling future prospects and draft capital.
Sweeney avoids short-term rentals, focusing instead on difference-makers with long-term contracts to aid playoff push.
The Bruins hold valuable draft picks and are prepared for trades to enhance roster, contingent on reasonable acquisition costs.
